explanations.1: This tells Make to use bash as the shell instead of the default sh, which gives you access to more advanced shell features.
explanations.3: This stores the number 33 in a variable called COLOR_YELLOW, which will be used later to make terminal text appear in yellow.
explanations.7: The \e[ and \e[0m are escape codes that tell your terminal to make text bold and yellow; the %s gets replaced with the message text.
explanations.18: The 'build' after 'deploy:' means that when you run 'make deploy', it will automatically run 'make build' first before doing the deploy step.
